title: Cyclostationarity: Core Theory
description: Cyclostationary processes, why modulated signals exhibit periodic statistics, the cyclic autocorrelation function, and the spectral correlation function (SCF).
objectives: Define cyclostationarity: a process whose statistics are periodic in time rather than constant
Explain why modulated signals (symbol clocking, carrier frequency, framing) are cyclostationary even when a naive PSD estimate makes them look plain stationary
Define the cyclic autocorrelation function and cycle frequency via the Fourier series expansion of R_x(t,τ) over t
Define the spectral correlation function (SCF) and explain why the ordinary PSD is blind to structure at nonzero cycle frequencies
